title: Large Compact Subvarieties of A_g
speaker: Jacob Tsimerman
abstract: (Joint with Samuel Grushevsky, Gabriele Mondello, Riccardo Salvati Manni) We determine the maximal dimension of a compact subvariety of the moduli space of principally polarized abelian varieties Ag for any value of g. For g
tags: Visions in Arithmetic and Beyond: Celebrating Peter Sarnak's Work and Impact

proof_stages.label: Compact-dimension invariants for quasi-projective varieties
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines the maximal dimension of compact subvarieties, its very-general-point version, and the basic boundary-codimension lower bound obtained by Bertini.

proof_stages.label: Specialization to \(A_g\) and statement of the theorem
proof_stages.summary: After recalling known characteristic-\(p\), Keel-Sadun, and Baily-Borel bounds for the moduli space of principally polarized abelian varieties, the speaker states the exact generic value \(g-1\) and the exact absolute maximal compact dimension.

proof_stages.label: Hecke-density remarks and Torelli applications
proof_stages.summary: The theorem is placed in context by explaining that Hecke translates make large compact subvarieties topologically dense and by deriving modest upper-bound consequences for \(M_g\), \(M_g^{ct}\), and the Torelli image.

proof_stages.label: Boundary deformation into the interior
proof_stages.summary: For a compact \(X\subset A_g\) of dimension at least \(g\), the decomposable locus \(A_1\times A_{g-1}\) is viewed as a deformation of the Baily-Borel boundary, so compactness forces its intersections with \(X\) to be nowhere of the expected dimension.

proof_stages.label: Hecke and lifted multifoliation amplification
proof_stages.summary: Hecke correspondences and then real symplectic translates on Siegel space enlarge the single boundary deformation to a dense family of leaves whose intersections with the lift of \(X\) can be forced to exist but remain unlikely.

proof_stages.label: Functional transcendence and weakly special explanations
proof_stages.summary: Ax-Schanuel/bi-algebraicity results for \(A_g\) say that algebraic intersections upstairs and downstairs that are too large must be accounted for by proper weakly special subvarieties.

proof_stages.label: Weakly-special reduction and Shimura optimization
proof_stages.summary: The weakly special obstruction gives the \(g-1\) bound through Hodge-generic points, classifies maximal compact subvarieties up to a compact Shimura factor and residual piece, and reduces the absolute formula to the classification of largest compact Shimura subvarieties of \(A_g\).

proof_stages.label: Characteristic-\(p\) generic variant via formal Ax
proof_stages.summary: Over \(\overline{\mathbb F}_p\) the generic compact-dimension question is reformulated through moving proper families, and the same \(g-1\) conclusion is shown to follow conditionally from a conjectural formal Ax/Tate-linearity type statement for completions of \(A_g\) at ordinary points.

examples.name: Characteristic-p no-p-torsion locus
examples.note: The locus over \(\overline{\mathbb F}_p\) of abelian varieties whose \(p\)-torsion is only the identity illustrates how much larger compact subvarieties can be in characteristic \(p\), but it is not used in the complex proof.
